start.speaker: fenrick
start.text: Well, well. The wanderer returns. I was starting to think you'd forgotten about me.

options.text: About the job — I'm working on it.
I'm not here about your job.
I've been thinking about your offer.
What's new in the shadows, Fenrick?

status_update.speaker: fenrick
status_update.text: Working on it. Right. And here I thought 'burn a building' was fairly straightforward. The guard shift change happens between second and third watch. That gap doesn't stay open forever.

status_pressure.speaker: fenrick
status_pressure.text: My employers are getting restless. They've got plans that depend on this — plans bigger than you or me or this rotting village. Time is running out. If you can't do it, say so, and I'll find someone who can.

options.text: I'll do it tonight. No more delays.
These 'plans' of your employers — what exactly happens after the granary burns?
I've changed my mind. I'm out.

tonight_response.speaker: fenrick
tonight_response.text: Good. Remember — the firebomb needs to be placed inside, near the grain stores. The flames need to spread before anyone can put them out. And for both our sakes, don't get caught.

bigger_picture.speaker: fenrick
bigger_picture.text: Let's just say the Hand has been waiting for the right moment to step out of the shadows. When the lord's last source of power goes up in smoke, the people will need someone to turn to. Someone who can actually feed them.

bigger_picture_2.speaker: fenrick
bigger_picture_2.text: We've been stockpiling supplies through... alternative channels. When the granary burns, we distribute food. Suddenly the Hand isn't a gang of thieves — we're saviors. The lord falls, and we rise. Elegant, no?

options.text: Trading one master for another. At least yours feeds people.
You're engineering a famine to seize power. That's monstrous.
You said the Hand has supply routes. What ELSE moves through them?

pragmatic_response.speaker: fenrick
pragmatic_response.text: Now you're thinking like us. Finish the job, and there'll be a place for you in the new Ashvale. A profitable place.

monster_response.speaker: fenrick
monster_response.text: Monstrous? The lord hoards grain while children starve. The priest preaches doom while buying alchemical supplies. I'm the honest one here — at least I admit what I want. Now... are you in or out?

job_complete.speaker: fenrick
job_complete.text: I saw the smoke from across the village. Beautiful work. Clean, fast — the Hand is impressed. Here's your gold, as promised.

job_complete_2.speaker: fenrick
job_complete_2.text: Phase two begins tonight. If you want more work — and more gold — come find me at the hideout behind the tannery after dark. Things are about to get very interesting in Ashvale.

refused_warning.speaker: fenrick
refused_warning.text: Then why ARE you here? Social call? I don't do those.

refused_warning_2.speaker: fenrick
refused_warning_2.text: A word of friendly advice: don't interfere with our business. The Hand has a long reach and a longer memory. You turned down the job — fine. But if I find out you've been talking to guards or that herbalist about our plans...

refused_warning_3.speaker: fenrick
refused_warning_3.text: Let's just say Ashvale is about to change. Dramatically. You can be on the winning side, or you can be in the way. Your choice. But time is running out to decide.

options.text: Maybe I was hasty. Tell me about the job again.
Threaten me again, Fenrick. See what happens.
I hear you. I'll stay out of it.

second_chance_offer.speaker: fenrick
second_chance_offer.text: Smart. The offer still stands — burn the granary, get paid. Firebomb, guard shift gap, don't get caught. Same deal as before. Two hundred gold. Take it or leave it.

options.text: I'll do it. Give me the firebomb.

stand_firm_response.speaker: fenrick
stand_firm_response.text: Brave. Stupid, but brave. We'll see how long that courage lasts when the real darkness comes to Ashvale. And it's coming. Sooner than you think.

bluff_followup.speaker: fenrick
bluff_followup.text: Thinking. Right. You've been 'thinking' for a while now. In my experience, people who think this long are either planning to say yes... or planning something else entirely.

bluff_followup_2.speaker: fenrick
bluff_followup_2.text: I hope for your sake it's the former. The Hand doesn't take kindly to people who waste our time. Or worse — people who plan to use our secrets against us.

general_info.speaker: fenrick
general_info.text: Things are moving. Commander Voss has been poking around the guild's territory — asking about shipments, supply routes. She's not just here for the crops. She's after something specific.

general_info_2.speaker: fenrick
general_info_2.text: And that priest — Brother Maren? He's been buying things. Candles and incense from the traders, sure. But also quicksilver and bone ash. That's not for prayer. That's for alchemy. Or something worse.

back_out_response.speaker: fenrick
back_out_response.text: Changed your mind? That's a dangerous habit in this line of work. The Hand doesn't forget commitments. Walk away if you want, but don't think this is over. You know too much now.

children_hint.speaker: fenrick
children_hint.text: He hesitates. For the first time since you met him, the smirk falters.

children_hint_2.speaker: fenrick
children_hint_2.text: Supplies. Mostly supplies. And... cargo that doesn't show up on any manifest. Small cargo. Cargo that cries at night if you don't keep it warm enough. Don't ask me more. Not here.
